product_overview: PEG is a local-first desktop app for composing high-aesthetic visual essays with per-character typography control, scene sequencing, and a canvas-based editor. It emphasizes granular lettering, scene/timeline control, and local asset management via a Tauri wrapper.
core_goals: Enable granular, per-character typography and animation (lettering) for visual essays
Provide a lightweight, responsive canvas editor for composing scenes with images, video and GIFs
Support local-first asset management with drag-and-drop workflow from user folders
Offer per-element timelines and scene sequencing with active pauses and simple transitions
Prepare for hardware-optimized exports via FFmpeg/Tauri (export integration planned)

features.name: Create Project
features.description: Create a new visual essay project from the Dashboard and open it in the Editor.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> See the PEG logo and "Novo Projeto" card on the dashboard -> Type a project name in the input field -> Click "Criar Projeto" button to create the project -> Get redirected to the Editor view
Navigate to / -> See the PEG logo and "Novo Projeto" card on the dashboard -> Leave project name empty -> Click "Criar Projeto" button -> See validation error or required-name prompt

features.name: Canvas Editor
features.description: Main editor where users compose visual scenes and manipulate images, videos, GIFs and text on a Fabric.js canvas synchronized with app state.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> See the editor layout with asset sidebar, canvas area and scene list at the bottom -> Drag and drop an image file from OS onto the canvas -> See the new element appear on the canvas -> Select the element -> Move and resize the element using Fabric.js handles -> See element properties synchronized with the Zustand project store

Navigate to / -> See the editor layout with asset sidebar, canvas area and scene list at the bottom -> Drag and drop an unsupported file type onto the canvas (e.g., .txt) -> See no element added or a rejected-file notification

features.name: Asset Sidebar
features.description: Sidebar for loading local asset folders (images, videos, GIFs), previewing filtered assets and dragging them onto the canvas.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Click "+ Pasta" button in the asset sidebar -> Select a local folder via file picker -> See filtered list of image/video/GIF files from the folder in the sidebar -> Drag an asset from the sidebar onto the canvas
Navigate to / -> Click "+ Pasta" button in the asset sidebar -> Cancel the folder selection dialog -> See no assets added to the sidebar or a "no folder selected" state

features.name: Scene Management
features.description: Create, reorder and navigate between scenes and add active pauses and transition types in the timeline.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> See the horizontal scene list at the bottom of the editor -> Click "+ Cena" to add a new scene -> Click a scene in the list to make it active -> See the canvas update to the active scene -> Drag a scene in the list to reorder it -> See scene order update

Navigate to / -> See the horizontal scene list at the bottom of the editor -> Click "+ Pausa" and choose "Tela Preta" to add a pause -> Click the pause block in the list -> Attempt to edit the pause duration -> See that pause duration is fixed (cannot be edited after creation)

features.name: Active Pauses
features.description: Insert visual pause blocks between scenes (black screen, VHS noise, solid color) and preview them on the canvas.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Click "+ Pausa" in the scene list -> Choose "VHS" from the pause menu -> Click the pause block in the scene list to preview it -> See VHS noise animation filling the canvas during preview

Navigate to / -> Click "+ Pausa" in the scene list -> Choose "Cor Sólida" to add a color pause -> Attempt to change the pause duration after creation -> See that duration cannot be modified (fixed default)

features.name: Element Timeline
features.description: Per-element tracks showing visibility windows (startTime/endTime) within a scene and handle-based editing of those times.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Add an image or text element to the active scene's canvas -> See the timeline panel appear below the canvas when the scene has elements -> See the element represented as a colored horizontal track -> Drag the left handle of the track to adjust startTime -> Drag the right handle of the track to adjust endTime -> See the element visibility update accordingly
Navigate to / -> Open a scene that contains no elements -> Observe that the timeline panel does not appear or shows an empty-state message

features.name: Lettering Engine
features.description: Advanced typography panel for adding text, choosing fonts and per-character animation; supports splitting text into individual character elements.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Click "✦ Lettering" button in the header to open the lettering panel -> Click "+ Texto" to add a text element to the canvas -> Type text into the text field -> Change font family via dropdown -> Adjust font size via slider -> Pick text color via color picker -> Select an animation effect (Tremor, Neon, Letreiro, Nenhum) -> Click "Split em letras individuais" -> See the text split into individual character elements on the canvas
Navigate to / -> Click "✦ Lettering" button in the header to open the lettering panel -> Click "+ Texto" when no scene is active -> See a prompt to select or create an active scene before adding text

routes.description: Root page — renders Dashboard if no project exists, otherwise renders the Editor

features.name: Create Project
features.description: User creates a new visual essay project from the Dashboard screen
user_interactions: See the PEG logo and "Novo Projeto" card on the dashboard
Type a project name in the input field
Click "Criar Projeto" button to create the project
Get redirected to the Editor view

features.name: Canvas Editor
features.description: Main editor where users compose visual scenes with images, videos, GIFs and text
user_interactions: See the editor layout with asset sidebar, canvas area and scene list at the bottom
Drag and drop image or video files onto the canvas
Select and move/resize elements on the canvas using Fabric.js handles
See elements synchronized with the Zustand project store

features.name: Asset Sidebar
features.description: Sidebar for loading local asset folders (images, videos, GIFs) and dragging them to the canvas
user_interactions: Click "+ Pasta" button to select a local folder
See filtered list of image/video/GIF files from the folder
Drag an asset from the sidebar onto the canvas

features.name: Scene Management
features.description: Create, reorder and navigate between scenes and active pauses in the timeline
user_interactions: See the horizontal scene list at the bottom of the editor
Click a scene to make it active (canvas updates)
Drag scenes to reorder them
Click "+ Cena" to add a new scene
Click "+ Pausa" and choose Tela Preta, VHS, or Cor Sólida to add an active pause
Click the CUT/FADE badge between scenes to toggle the transition type

features.name: Active Pauses
features.description: Visual pause blocks between scenes (black screen, VHS noise, solid color)
user_interactions: Add a pause via "+ Pausa" menu in the scene list
Click the pause block to preview it in the canvas area
See VHS noise animation or black/solid color filling the canvas

features.name: Element Timeline
features.description: Per-element tracks showing visibility window (startTime/endTime) within a scene
user_interactions: See the timeline panel appear below the canvas when a scene has elements
See each element as a colored horizontal track
Drag the left handle to adjust startTime
Drag the right handle to adjust endTime

features.name: Lettering Engine
features.description: Advanced typography panel for adding and animating text with per-character control
user_interactions: Click "✦ Lettering" button in the header to open the lettering panel
Click "+ Texto" to add a text element to the canvas
Change font family via dropdown
Adjust font size via slider
Pick text color via color picker
Select animation effect (Tremor, Neon, Letreiro, Nenhum)
Click "Split em letras individuais" to break text into individual character elements

known_limitations.issue: Tauri bridge (JS <-> Rust) is only tested with a mock greet command; FFmpeg export is not yet implemented
known_limitations.impact: Export button in the header is non-functional (Phase 5 pending)

known_limitations.issue: Active Pauses cannot have their duration edited after creation
known_limitations.impact: Pause duration is fixed at 3s default

known_limitations.issue: GIF support via gifler uses a fallback timeout of 500ms for first-frame resolution
known_limitations.impact: Very large GIFs may not render on the first attempt
